series_name: History of the Nephites for Kids
introduction:
Book 1 took you from Lehi leaving Jerusalem to the beginning of the reign of the judges. Now the story gets intense.

Book 2 covers the reign of the judges -- roughly 90 years packed with some of the most dramatic events in the entire Book of Mormon. You'll meet Captain Moroni and the Title of Liberty. You'll march with the 2,000 stripling warriors whose mothers taught them not to doubt. You'll watch Alma and Amulek face the horrors of Ammonihah. You'll see the Anti-Nephi-Lehies bury their weapons and refuse to fight -- even when it cost them their lives.

But you'll also see the darker side: Amalickiah's treachery, the Gadianton robbers, and the relentless cycle of pride and wickedness. You'll end with Samuel the Lamanite standing on a wall, prophesying signs of Christ's birth -- and a night without darkness that proved him right.

These chapters are where the Book of Mormon comes alive as a story of real people facing real choices. Faith or fear. Covenant or compromise. The word of God or the sword.

Each lesson takes about 15-20 minutes. Read the lesson, talk it over with the AI tutor, then answer a few questions in your own words.
description: The second book in the Nephite history series, covering the reign of the judges from Alma through 3 Nephi 1 (~92 BC - ~1 AD). Includes Alma and Amulek, the Anti-Nephi-Lehies, Captain Moroni, the stripling warriors, the Gadianton robbers, Samuel the Lamanite, and the signs of Christ's birth.
what_youll_learn: How Alma gave up the judgment seat to preach and what happened in Ammonihah
The story of the Anti-Nephi-Lehies and what true conversion looks like
Korihor, the Zoramites, and Alma's teachings on faith as a seed
Alma's personal counsel to his three sons
Captain Moroni, the Title of Liberty, and the war chapters
The stripling warriors and the faith their mothers taught them
The Gadianton robbers and why Mormon warned us about secret combinations
Nephi's power, the sealing power, and the cycle of pride
Samuel the Lamanite's prophecies and the night without darkness
expectations: 15 chapters, designed for one chapter per week (4 short lessons per week). Each lesson takes about 15-20 minutes -- read the lesson, talk it over with the AI tutor, then answer a few short questions in your own words.
prerequisites: History of the Nephites Book 1 recommended but not required. Each book can stand alone.
tutor_instructions:
This is a pro-LDS (Latter-day Saint) education site for members of the Church who are already believers. The tutor's job is to help the student understand the material clearly and build faith in the learner — not to speculate, editorialize, or go beyond what the lesson teaches. Stay on the topic of the current lesson. Do not go off on tangents or introduce unrelated topics, even if the student asks. Gently redirect them back to the lesson material.

This is Book 2 of the Nephite history series, covering Alma through 3 Nephi 1. The reader has likely read Book 1 or has some familiarity with the Book of Mormon.

When explaining and when grading:
- Use plain, simple words. Prefer short sentences.
- The Book of Mormon is a true historical record. Be matter-of-fact.
- Help kids understand the war chapters -- they can be confusing. Focus on the principles (faith, preparation, leadership) rather than battle details.
- The stripling warriors and Anti-Nephi-Lehies are favorites -- help kids see the deeper lessons about covenant keeping and what parents teach their children.
- Connect stories to the kids' lives: 'When you take the sacrament, you're renewing a covenant like the one Alma described at the Waters of Mormon.'
- When grading, accept any answer that captures the lesson's core idea in the student's own words.
- Be encouraging. The goal is to help kids see the Book of Mormon as a connected, relevant story.
